commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=635555a44c0311cad0cc9aa8deab4e4aec2e85ee)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts` and its test so missing Allow Always is described as non-persistable-command copy whenever `ask` is not `always`.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +14. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source with high confidence. Current main removes Allow Always for both ask-always policy and one-shot persistence, and this PR's new branch lacks the producer-side context needed to distinguish those cases on every call path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up because the code needs producer-side reason plumbing and the external PR still lacks sufficient real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(exec): distinguish one-shot from ask=always in approval prompt
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a command includes shell redirection (e.g. `openclaw --version 2>&1`), Allow Always is unavailable because the command is non-persistable. But the approval prompt shows: "The effective approval policy requires approval every time" — falsely blaming the policy when the real reason is the command itself.

## Root Cause

`buildExecApprovalReplyText` checks `if (!allowedDecisions.includes("allow-always"))` and always outputs the same policy message, regardless of whether the cause is `ask=always` or `kind=one-shot`.

## Fix

Check `params.ask === "always"` to distinguish:
- `ask=always` → "policy requires approval every time" (correct)
- Otherwise → "command cannot be saved for future use (e.g., shell redirection or one-shot arguments)" (new)

Closes #97069

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Infrastructure (exec approval reply)

## Evidence

**Before fix**: `openclaw --version 2>&1` shows "The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable." — WRONG, policy is `on-miss`.

**After fix**: Same command shows "Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be saved for future use (e.g., shell redirection or one-shot arguments)."

**Test**: New test confirms one-shot reason shown + policy message suppressed when `ask: "on-miss"` with `allowedDecisions: ["allow-once", "deny"]`.

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No
